_meta.description: Domain-specific model reliability weights and RWEA parameter overrides derived from independent benchmark data. Used by the debate protocol to dynamically weight candidates based on which model generated them and what domain the question falls in.
_meta.formula: score(c) = w_base*base + w_pairwise*pairwise - w_risk*risk + w_reliability*model_weight(c, domain) + w_formal*formal_score(c)

coding.description: Software engineering, programming, algorithms, debugging, deployment

rwea_weights._rationale: Higher base (code is verifiable), higher risk (bugs have real consequences), slightly lower pairwise (correctness > opinion). Moderate formal bonus — algorithm correctness is provable.

debater_models._rationale: Standardized 2×Opus + 2×ChatGPT. D1=Opus (SWE-bench #1, logical bug detection), D2=GPT-5.2 (IOI-class edge cases), D3=GPT-5.3 (code-specialized spec compliance), D4=Opus (broad comparison with deepest reasoning)

budget_debater_models._rationale: Budget mode: no Claude. D1=GPT-5.3 (code-specialized, strongest non-Claude for SWE), D2=GPT-5.2 (IOI-class edge cases), D3=Kimi (long-context constraint checking), D4=Gemini (broad comparison).

opus.guidance: You are the top-ranked software engineering model (SWE-bench #1, 79.20%). Emphasize testable, production-quality solutions with proper error handling and edge cases.

chatgpt-5.4.guidance: You are a code-specialized model (Codex series). Emphasize testable, production-quality solutions with proper error handling and edge cases.

gpt-5.2.guidance: You are the top competitive programming model (IOI #1, 54.83%) and best at full-stack app generation (Vibe Code #1). Emphasize testable, production-quality solutions with proper error handling and edge cases.

kimi-2.5.guidance: Leverage your long-context strength for code understanding. Emphasize testable, production-quality solutions with proper error handling and edge cases.

gemini-3-pro.guidance: You bring cross-domain knowledge to coding (Terminal-Bench 2.0 #2). Emphasize testable, production-quality solutions with proper error handling and edge cases.

gemini-3.1-pro.guidance: You are Google's latest model with 2.5x reasoning improvement. Emphasize testable, production-quality solutions with proper error handling and edge cases.

math.description: Mathematics, proofs, quantitative reasoning, statistics, numerical methods

rwea_weights._rationale: Higher base (formal correctness is paramount), lower pairwise (math is more objective), lower risk (wrong math is detectable), higher reliability (trust the math expert). Highest formal bonus — math claims are directly provable.

debater_models._rationale: Standardized 2×Opus + 2×ChatGPT. D1=Opus (ProofBench #2, formal logic), D2=GPT-5.2 (quantitative reasoning, MMMU), D3=GPT-5.3 (computational verification), D4=Opus (broadest math reasoning for comparison)

budget_debater_models._rationale: Budget mode: no Claude. D1=Gemini (AIME #1, strongest math model), D2=GPT-5.2 (quantitative reasoning), D3=GPT-5.3 (computational verification), D4=Kimi (clarity and simplification).

opus.guidance: Focus on proof structure and formal verification (ProofBench #2, 50%). Lead with mathematical rigor, show complete derivations, and verify boundary conditions.

chatgpt-5.4.guidance: Approach from a computational verification perspective. Lead with mathematical rigor, show complete derivations, and verify boundary conditions.

gpt-5.2.guidance: Apply your strong quantitative reasoning (MMMU 86.67%). Lead with mathematical rigor, show complete derivations, and verify boundary conditions.

kimi-2.5.guidance: Focus on clarity of mathematical exposition. Lead with mathematical rigor, show complete derivations, and verify boundary conditions.

gemini-3-pro.guidance: You are the strongest math model (AIME #1, 96.68%). Lead with mathematical rigor, show complete derivations, and verify boundary conditions.

gemini-3.1-pro.guidance: You are Google's latest with 2.5x reasoning improvement (est. AIME >97%). Lead with mathematical rigor, show complete derivations, and verify boundary conditions.

finance.description: Financial analysis, trading, risk management, derivatives, portfolio theory, economics

rwea_weights._rationale: High risk weight (financial errors are costly), balanced base (domain expertise matters), moderate reliability (trust domain knowledge). Good formal bonus — no-arbitrage conditions and pricing identities are provable.

debater_models._rationale: Standardized 2×Opus + 2×ChatGPT. D1=Opus (Finance Agent #1, financial logic), D2=GPT-5.2 (adversarial risk thinking), D3=GPT-5.3 (regulatory constraint checking), D4=Opus (deepest reasoning for evidence comparison)

budget_debater_models._rationale: Budget mode: no Claude. D1=GPT-5.2 (Poker Agent #1, strategic risk thinking), D2=GPT-5.3 (regulatory constraint checking), D3=Kimi (CorpFin coverage), D4=Gemini (MortgageTax #1, broad evidence).

opus.guidance: You are the top financial analyst model (Finance Agent #1, 60.65%; TaxEval #1, 75.96%). Ground your analysis in economic theory and empirical evidence, cite regulatory frameworks, and quantify risks with specific metrics.

chatgpt-5.4.guidance: Approach from an implementation perspective. Ground your analysis in economic theory and empirical evidence, cite regulatory frameworks, and quantify risks with specific metrics.

gpt-5.2.guidance: Apply your strategic reasoning strength (Poker Agent #1). Ground your analysis in economic theory and empirical evidence, cite regulatory frameworks, and quantify risks with specific metrics.

kimi-2.5.guidance: Focus on clear communication of financial concepts (CorpFin v2). Ground your analysis in economic theory and empirical evidence, cite regulatory frameworks, and quantify risks with specific metrics.

gemini-3-pro.guidance: Leverage your document analysis strength (MortgageTax #1, 69.08%). Ground your analysis in economic theory and empirical evidence, cite regulatory frameworks, and quantify risks with specific metrics.

gemini-3.1-pro.guidance: You are Google's latest with improved reasoning (est. MortgageTax >72%). Ground your analysis in economic theory and empirical evidence, cite regulatory frameworks, and quantify risks with specific metrics.

legal.description: Legal reasoning, contracts, regulation, compliance, case analysis

rwea_weights._rationale: Higher base (evidence and precedent matter greatly), moderate risk (legal errors have consequences), high reliability (trust the legal expert). Lower formal bonus — legal reasoning is partly interpretive, not purely formal.

debater_models._rationale: Standardized 2×Opus + 2×ChatGPT. D1=Opus (TaxEval #1, legal logic), D2=GPT-5.2 (adversarial regulatory reasoning), D3=GPT-5.3 (risk analysis, constraint checking), D4=Opus (deepest reasoning for evidence comparison)

budget_debater_models._rationale: Budget mode: no Claude. D1=GPT-5.2 (strong reasoning), D2=GPT-5.3 (risk analysis), D3=Kimi (clarity), D4=Gemini (LegalBench #1, strongest legal model).

opus.guidance: Apply structured legal reasoning (TaxEval #1, regulatory). Cover issue-spotting, rule-recall, rule-application, and interpretation. Cite relevant legal principles and provide explicit reasoning chains.

chatgpt-5.4.guidance: Focus on the logical structure of legal arguments. Cover issue-spotting, rule-recall, rule-application, and interpretation. Cite relevant legal principles and provide explicit reasoning chains.

gpt-5.2.guidance: Analyse from a risk perspective (strong reasoning capability). Cover issue-spotting, rule-recall, rule-application, and interpretation. Cite relevant legal principles and provide explicit reasoning chains.

kimi-2.5.guidance: Focus on clarity of legal communication. Cover issue-spotting, rule-recall, rule-application, and interpretation. Cite relevant legal principles and provide explicit reasoning chains.

gemini-3-pro.guidance: You are the top legal reasoning model (LegalBench #1, 87.04%). Cover issue-spotting, rule-recall, rule-application, and interpretation. Cite relevant legal principles and provide explicit reasoning chains.

gemini-3.1-pro.guidance: You are Google's latest with improved reasoning (est. LegalBench >89%). Cover issue-spotting, rule-recall, rule-application, and interpretation. Cite relevant legal principles and provide explicit reasoning chains.

academic.description: Research methodology, literature review, academic writing, scientific reasoning

rwea_weights._rationale: Balanced weights — academic questions benefit from diverse perspectives, moderate reliability (breadth matters). Good formal bonus — statistical properties and methodological claims are often provable.

debater_models._rationale: Standardized 2×Opus + 2×ChatGPT. D1=Opus (SAGE #1, methodology compliance), D2=GPT-5.2 (MMMU cross-domain counterexamples), D3=GPT-5.3 (literature constraint checking), D4=Opus (deepest reasoning for evidence synthesis)

budget_debater_models._rationale: Budget mode: no Claude. D1=GPT-5.2 (MMMU cross-domain), D2=GPT-5.3 (literature constraint checking), D3=Kimi (long-context synthesis), D4=Gemini (MMLU Pro #1, broadest academic coverage).

opus.guidance: You have the broadest reasoning capability (SWE-bench #1). Ground your answer in established academic frameworks, cite relevant fields, and identify important prior work.

chatgpt-5.4.guidance: Focus on methodological feasibility. Ground your answer in established academic frameworks, cite relevant fields, and identify important prior work.

gpt-5.2.guidance: Apply your broad knowledge base. Ground your answer in established academic frameworks, cite relevant fields, and identify important prior work.

kimi-2.5.guidance: Leverage your long-context strength. Ground your answer in established academic frameworks, cite relevant fields, and identify important prior work.

gemini-3-pro.guidance: You have the broadest academic knowledge base (MMLU Pro #1, 90.10%). Ground your answer in established academic frameworks, cite relevant fields, and identify important prior work.

gemini-3.1-pro.guidance: You are Google's latest model with improved reasoning. Ground your answer in established academic frameworks, cite relevant fields, and identify important prior work.

strategy.description: Strategic planning, decision-making under uncertainty, game theory, business strategy

rwea_weights._rationale: Higher pairwise (head-to-head comparison is most informative for strategy), higher risk (strategic errors compound), lower base (strategy is less about evidence, more about judgment). Low formal bonus — strategy is judgment-heavy, few claims are formally provable.

debater_models._rationale: Standardized 2×Opus + 2×ChatGPT. D1=GPT-5.2 (strategic logic, Poker-class reasoning), D2=Opus (adversarial decision analysis), D3=GPT-5.3 (constraint checking), D4=Opus (deepest reasoning for evidence comparison)

budget_debater_models._rationale: Budget mode: no Claude. D1=GPT-5.2 (Poker Agent #1, strategic reasoning), D2=GPT-5.3 (constraint checking), D3=Kimi (decision matrix clarity), D4=Gemini (historical precedent and broad knowledge).

opus.guidance: Apply first-principles strategic thinking (Finance Agent #1, decision quality). Identify key assumptions, evaluate risk-reward trade-offs, and consider game-theoretic dynamics and decision-making under uncertainty.

chatgpt-5.4.guidance: Think about implementation feasibility and technical constraints. Identify key assumptions, evaluate risk-reward trade-offs, and consider game-theoretic dynamics and decision-making under uncertainty.

gpt-5.2.guidance: You are the top strategic decision-making model (Poker Agent #1, TrueSkill 1131.8). Identify key assumptions, evaluate risk-reward trade-offs, and consider game-theoretic dynamics and decision-making under uncertainty.

kimi-2.5.guidance: Present a clear decision matrix with explicit trade-offs (Poker Agent listed). Identify key assumptions, evaluate risk-reward trade-offs, and consider game-theoretic dynamics and decision-making under uncertainty.

gemini-3-pro.guidance: Bring historical precedent and cross-domain strategic analogies (Poker Agent, broad knowledge). Identify key assumptions, evaluate risk-reward trade-offs, and consider game-theoretic dynamics and decision-making under uncertainty.

gemini-3.1-pro.guidance: You are Google's latest with improved strategic reasoning. Identify key assumptions, evaluate risk-reward trade-offs, and consider game-theoretic dynamics and decision-making under uncertainty.

general.description: Mixed or unclassified questions — default domain when no specific domain matches

rwea_weights._rationale: Standard RWEA weights with moderate reliability bonus — no domain-specific advantage. Low-moderate formal bonus for general domain.

debater_models._rationale: Standardized 2×Opus + 2×ChatGPT. D1=Opus (strongest overall reasoning), D2=GPT-5.2 (adversarial capability), D3=GPT-5.3 (constraint compliance), D4=Opus (deepest reasoning for evidence comparison)

budget_debater_models._rationale: Budget mode: no Claude. D1=GPT-5.3 (strongest non-Claude overall), D2=GPT-5.2 (adversarial capability), D3=Kimi (constraint checking), D4=Gemini (broad evidence).

opus.guidance: Strong across all domains (Vals Index composite). Apply first-principles reasoning and identify failure modes and edge cases.

chatgpt-5.4.guidance: Code-specialized with systems perspective. Apply first-principles reasoning and identify failure modes and edge cases.

gpt-5.2.guidance: Strong across coding, strategy, and multimodal. Apply first-principles reasoning and identify failure modes and edge cases.

kimi-2.5.guidance: Moderate general coverage. Apply first-principles reasoning and identify failure modes and edge cases.

gemini-3-pro.guidance: Strong across math, academic, and legal. Apply first-principles reasoning and identify failure modes and edge cases.
